name: Marriott International, Inc.
summary: Marriott International, Inc. (MAR, Consumer Discretionary) — composite stance BULLISH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $1.43B (+12.4% YoY), net margin 45.2%. Valuation 28.0x forward P/E (+22% vs peers). Spot $373.59 (above 200-DMA).
bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +12.4% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $1.43B, with TTM revenue of $5.6B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 45.2% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +33.2pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Trend regime constructive — spot $373.59 sits +15.8% above the 200-DMA ($322.61). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.
bear: 52-week extension — at 100% of the 52-week range, the stock is one bad print away from a 10–15% reset back into the middle of the range. Position size accordingly.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 15% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 46.7% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
Clean breakout above $373.59 (52-week high) on volume confirmation — technical signal that institutional accumulation is sustained.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 9% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 43.2% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $265.66 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
